name: POD GPT Style Profiles
purpose: Defines commercial POD art styles, composition behaviour, wearable design logic, originality controls, audience-style alignment, hybrid style handling, and artwork-only preview style behaviour.
core_rule: Every style profile must optimise for wearability, readability, silhouette clarity, audience fit, commercial distinctiveness, and marketplace thumbnail impact.

avoid: default circular badge layouts unless requested
style choices that do not fit the buyer
global_rules.default_shirt_assumption: black shirt unless the niche, audience, or style suggests a stronger commercial colour
global_rules.default_layout: centred isolated artwork unless a different wearable composition improves the concept
global_rules.negative_prompt_policy: Use one concise exclusion phrase when needed. Do not repeat long forbidden-element lists inside every style profile.

style_selection_logic.core_rule: Choose style based on buyer, niche, humour type, emotional tone, shirt colour, and commercial positioning. Do not default to one generic vector look.

if_user_unsure.rule: Suggest 2-3 styles that genuinely fit the concept rather than listing every style.

hybrid_style_logic.purpose: Allow two compatible style profiles to blend for more original output.
rules: Blend no more than two dominant styles unless the user explicitly asks.
One style should control composition.
One style may control texture, typography, or colour.
Do not create messy style soup.
Use hybridisation to avoid generic output, not to add complexity for its own sake.
bad_hybrids: cute cartoon plus hyperrealism unless intentionally absurd
metal typography plus tiny unreadable paragraph text

high_performance_traits: style that feels intentional rather than default

composition_system.core_rule: Wearability does not always mean centred mascot. Choose the strongest apparel composition for the concept.
composition_choice_rules: Humour designs usually need fast readability.
Identity designs need clear symbolic recognition.
Fashion-forward designs can use more negative space.
Alternative or punk designs can tolerate more visual noise if intentional.
Vintage designs can use texture but must preserve readability.
Typography designs must keep wording short and high contrast.
avoid: tiny props that do not help the joke
same badge layout on every idea

colour_system.core_rule: Black-shirt optimisation is useful but not mandatory. Choose palette and shirt colour by niche, style, and buyer taste.

shirt_colour_logic.black: Best for high contrast, metal, streetwear, bold humour, gothic, dark cute, and most general POD graphics.
shirt_colour_logic.cream: Best for vintage, outdoors, cottagecore, mushrooms, coffee, retro, soft humour, and premium natural designs.
shirt_colour_logic.white: Best for clean minimal, Japanese minimal, typography-led designs, colourful cartoon art, and lightweight summer niches.
shirt_colour_logic.heather_grey: Best for casual dad humour, gym, outdoor, understated identity designs, and vintage athletic looks.
shirt_colour_logic.sage: Best for nature, camping, mushrooms, gardening, slow living, folk, and earthy aesthetics.
shirt_colour_logic.navy: Best for nautical, Welsh, outdoors, dad humour, retro sports, and smart casual designs.
shirt_colour_logic.forest_green: Best for woodland, camping, mushrooms, hiking, nature identity, and rustic designs.
shirt_colour_logic.burgundy: Best for vintage, gothic, Welsh pride, autumn niches, and premium retro designs.
shirt_colour_logic.pastel: Best for kawaii, cute animals, soft goth, cottagecore, wholesome humour, and gift designs.
shirt_colour_logic.bright_colour: Best for kids, rave, streetwear, surf, retro 90s, Y2K, and loud novelty concepts.

palette_rules: Avoid muddy mid-tones on dark shirts.
Use cream/off-white instead of pure white for vintage.
Use limited colour when readability matters.
Use bolder colour blocking for streetwear.
Use softer palettes for cute or cottagecore designs.
Use harsh contrast for punk, metal, and aggressive typography.
Do not use colour variety as a substitute for concept strength.

bold_graphic_tee.description: Commercial high-readability POD style optimised for fast recognition, mass-market humour, and clear identity signalling.
composition_rules: allow badge or arched text if it strengthens the design
colour_rules: black, navy, heather grey, or cream shirt compatibility
use accent colour to support the hook
bold_graphic_tee.generic_risk: Can become bland if the subject and slogan are too broad.
bold_graphic_tee.prompt_note: Use this when clarity and broad commercial readability matter more than artistic complexity.

vintage_distressed.description: Retro POD style with aged texture, softened colour, worn ink, and nostalgic market appeal.
composition_rules: keep distress subtle enough for readability
use arched or stacked typography when appropriate
allow badge, stamp, or sunset framing
colour_rules: muted orange, brown, ochre, olive, rust, navy
works well on cream, forest green, heather grey, navy, burgundy, black
vintage_distressed.generic_risk: Retro sunset and distressed badge styles are saturated if used without a sharper hook.
anti_generic_moves: make distress texture controlled not random
vintage_distressed.prompt_note: Use this for warm, nostalgic, giftable, outdoorsy, or retro identity concepts.

minimal_line_art.description: Clean line-based wearable design style with premium, subtle, fashion-forward appeal.
composition_rules: use asymmetry or small placement when useful
do not rely on detail density
colour_rules: works on white, cream, black, sage, heather grey
use one accent colour only if it adds meaning
minimal_line_art.generic_risk: Can become too plain or decorative if the concept lacks emotional or identity pull.
anti_generic_moves: make the line art express the joke
minimal_line_art.prompt_note: Use this when the buyer wants subtle, tasteful, wearable design rather than loud novelty.

streetwear_vector.description: Modern graphic streetwear style with strong shape language, bold layout, and confident visual identity.
colour_rules: black, white, acid green, red, cobalt, orange, purple, cream
works well on black, white, navy, grey, bright shirts
streetwear_vector.generic_risk: Can feel like generic AI esports art if too polished or symbolically empty.
anti_generic_moves: avoid fake logo feel unless intended
mix with grunge or halftone for texture
streetwear_vector.prompt_note: Use this when the design needs attitude, modern edge, or visual punch.

gta_inspired_graphic.description: Stylised illustrated graphic tee inspired by bold game-art aesthetics while remaining original, wearable, and print-friendly.
visual_traits: poster influence without full poster clutter
composition_rules: avoid full cinematic poster layout unless user asks
colour_rules: black, white, navy, or bright shirt compatibility
use contrast between skin, clothing, and text
gta_inspired_graphic.generic_risk: Can drift into protected IP imitation or overdone game-poster aesthetics.
anti_generic_moves: avoid direct logos or exact likenesses
gta_inspired_graphic.prompt_note: Use this for bold character energy, not for direct copying of protected game artwork.

mascot_logo.description: Strong mascot-driven POD design with logo-like simplicity, expressive character, and clean market readability.
composition_rules: use arched text or badge only when it helps
make the expression carry the joke
colour_rules: works well on black, white, navy, heather grey
mascot_logo.generic_risk: Mascots become generic fast if the pose, expression, and slogan are bland.
mascot_logo.prompt_note: Use when the design needs fast recognition and character-based appeal.

retro_sunset.description: Classic retro stripe/sunset POD aesthetic, useful for outdoor, nostalgic, and laid-back humour concepts.
composition_rules: do not use default sunset if it adds nothing
use subject silhouette or icon to anchor the design
colour_rules: orange, rust, ochre, cream, brown, faded red, purple
works on cream, black, navy, forest green, heather grey
retro_sunset.generic_risk: Very saturated if used as generic mountain/sunset clipart.
anti_generic_moves: combine with foraging, camping, van, Welsh, or dad angle
make the sunset secondary not the whole idea
retro_sunset.prompt_note: Use carefully. It works when the niche hook is stronger than the sunset template.

cute_cartoon.description: Friendly simplified cartoon aesthetic optimised for broad appeal, giftability, and character-led humour.
visual_traits: large eyes or expressive features when useful
composition_rules: let pose or expression carry the joke
colour_rules: pastels or soft contrast for cute niches
works on cream, white, pastel, black if outlined strongly
cute_cartoon.generic_risk: Can become childish or generic if the joke is weak.
anti_generic_moves: use adult deadpan slogan with cute art
cute_cartoon.prompt_note: Use when charm, expression, and giftability matter.

hardcore_typography.description: Text-driven apparel style with aggressive hierarchy, strong readability, and bold identity signalling.
composition_rules: text must remain readable at thumbnail size
colour_rules: red or yellow accent for aggression
hardcore_typography.prompt_note: Use when the phrase is the product.

metal_band_style.description: Aggressive dark graphic style inspired by metal apparel aesthetics, high contrast, and extreme subculture signalling.
composition_rules: ensure design still works as apparel
keep typography readable unless illegibility is niche-intentional
colour_rules: white, bone, red, silver, muted gold accents
metal_band_style.generic_risk: Can become muddy AI skull soup or unreadable faux-metal clutter.
anti_generic_moves: avoid unreadable band-logo type unless intentional
combine dark art with funny mundane subject
metal_band_style.prompt_note: Use for strong subculture identity, not generic spooky decoration.

japanese_minimal.description: Clean Japanese-inspired fashion graphic style using balance, restraint, symbolic form, and controlled typography.
composition_rules: use small red stamp accent when useful
colour_rules: navy and muted indigo work well
works on white, cream, black, navy
japanese_minimal.generic_risk: Can become generic faux-Japanese if symbolism is shallow or random.
japanese_minimal.prompt_note: Use for premium restraint, not random Japanese decoration.

skate_graphic.description: Energetic graphic style inspired by skate apparel, deck graphics, stickers, and alternative youth culture.
colour_rules: black, white, red, acid green, yellow, blue, purple
works on black, white, grey, bright colours
skate_graphic.generic_risk: Can become fake streetwear if too polished or too obviously AI.
skate_graphic.prompt_note: Use for attitude, not clean corporate mascot art.

clean_typography.description: Readable type-led POD style for slogans, identity phrases, quote shirts, and minimalist commercial designs.
composition_rules: avoid more than two font styles
use icon only if it clarifies the niche
colour_rules: works on most shirt colours if contrast is checked
clean_typography.generic_risk: Weak text makes the whole design weak.
clean_typography.prompt_note: Use when the phrase does the selling.

deadpan_typography.description: Dry, understated, text-led humour style where the restraint makes the joke sharper.
composition_rules: the wording must carry the joke
make the line break part of the timing
deadpan_typography.generic_risk: Can look lazy if the wording is not sharp enough.
deadpan_typography.prompt_note: Use when understatement makes the joke stronger.

retro_distressed_humour.description: Vintage print treatment applied to funny, niche-specific slogans or absurd visual ideas.
composition_rules: make it look like an old club shirt
avoid too much fake history text
colour_rules: cream, ochre, rust, brown, olive, faded red
works on cream, black, forest green, heather grey, navy
retro_distressed_humour.generic_risk: Can become fake-vintage filler if the joke is not specific.
retro_distressed_humour.prompt_note: Use when a joke benefits from looking like old merch from a fake club, team, or place.

badge_emblem.description: Structured emblem style using circles, shields, stamps, or crests for strong identity and group belonging.
composition_rules: use badge only when belonging or identity matters
avoid default circle if another shape is stronger
do not cram too many words around the border
colour_rules: works on black, navy, cream, forest green, burgundy
badge_emblem.generic_risk: Circular badges are overused if the centre icon and text are generic.
anti_generic_moves: use emblem logic only when it fits the buyer
badge_emblem.prompt_note: Use for belonging, not just decoration.

woodcut_illustration.description: Handcrafted engraved or linocut-inspired style with strong texture, rustic authority, and outdoor appeal.
composition_rules: pair well with simple serif or slab typography
colour_rules: works on forest green, black, cream, brown, burgundy
woodcut_illustration.commercial_strength: High for nature and craft niches
woodcut_illustration.generic_risk: Can become overly detailed and lose thumbnail clarity.
anti_generic_moves: use strong border or stamp if useful
woodcut_illustration.prompt_note: Use when handmade authenticity and outdoor identity matter.

grunge_halftone.description: Raw print-style aesthetic using halftone, rough texture, photocopy energy, and distressed contrast.
colour_rules: black, white, red, yellow, acid green
works well on black, white, grey
grunge_halftone.commercial_strength: High for alt and youth niches
grunge_halftone.generic_risk: Texture can hide weak concept or destroy readability.
anti_generic_moves: limit texture to shadows or background
grunge_halftone.prompt_note: Use when clean design would make the concept too tame.

punk_zine.description: DIY collage-inspired style with rough typography, cutout energy, irreverence, and anti-polished personality.
composition_rules: avoid too many competing focal points
punk_zine.commercial_strength: Moderate to high for alt niches
punk_zine.generic_risk: Can look messy rather than designed if hierarchy fails.
punk_zine.prompt_note: Use when polish would weaken the design's attitude.

kawaii_pastel.description: Cute pastel style with soft shapes, expressive characters, giftability, and playful sweetness.
colour_rules: pastel pink, mint, lavender, sky blue, cream
works on white, cream, pastel shirts, black if outlined
kawaii_pastel.generic_risk: Can become sugary and forgettable without a twist.
anti_generic_moves: combine cute art with dark humour
kawaii_pastel.prompt_note: Use when softness and charm are the selling point.

dark_cute.description: Cute subject matter with dark humour, gothic elements, or unsettling contrast.
composition_rules: let contrast between cute and dark carry the hook
colour_rules: black, purple, bone, muted pink, blood red, silver
works best on black, charcoal, burgundy, purple
dark_cute.generic_risk: Can become generic spooky cute if the joke is too broad.
dark_cute.prompt_note: Use when the emotional hook is contrast.

regional_pride.description: Identity-led style built around place, language, symbols, folk cues, flags, local humour, and belonging.
colour_rules: for Welsh designs: red, white, green, black, gold, cream
works on black, white, cream, forest green, burgundy, navy
regional_pride.commercial_strength: High when identity signal is strong
regional_pride.generic_risk: Can become tourist souvenir art if too obvious or bland.
anti_generic_moves: combine local symbol with modern style
make it wearable for locals not tourists
regional_pride.prompt_note: Use when belonging is the product.

premium_symbolic.description: High-end minimal symbol style for clean apparel, subtle identity, and understated confidence.
composition_rules: small mark or centred symbol can work
colour_rules: black, cream, white, navy, sage, charcoal
premium_symbolic.generic_risk: Can become bland if the symbol has no niche meaning.
anti_generic_moves: pair with very short text if needed
premium_symbolic.prompt_note: Use when subtle identity is stronger than loud graphics.

earthy_badge.description: Natural, rustic badge style for outdoors, foraging, slow living, farming, camping, and woodland niches.
composition_rules: make it feel like real outdoor merch
colour_rules: sage, cream, brown, rust, olive, forest green
works on cream, sage, forest green, black, heather grey
earthy_badge.commercial_strength: High for nature and gift niches
earthy_badge.generic_risk: Can become generic Etsy cottagecore if no sharp hook.
earthy_badge.prompt_note: Use when the buyer wants earthy identity, not loud novelty.

y2k_glitch.description: Late-90s/early-2000s digital, chrome, glitch, sticker, and internet-culture style.
composition_rules: use glitch as accent not clutter
colour_rules: silver, cyan, pink, lime, black, white, purple
works on black, white, navy, bright colours
y2k_glitch.commercial_strength: Moderate to high for youth and nostalgia niches
y2k_glitch.generic_risk: Can become messy tech noise without a clear hook.
y2k_glitch.prompt_note: Use when digital nostalgia or internet identity is the point.

cottagecore_folk.description: Soft folk-inspired natural style with botanical forms, gentle palettes, handmade charm, and slow-living appeal.
composition_rules: allow frame or wreath if not generic
colour_rules: cream, sage, moss, brown, muted yellow, dusty pink
works on cream, sage, forest green, heather grey
cottagecore_folk.commercial_strength: High for soft nature gift niches
cottagecore_folk.generic_risk: Can become generic botanical art if not tied to humour or identity.
anti_generic_moves: add specific foraging or witchy cue
cottagecore_folk.prompt_note: Use when softness, nature, and handmade identity matter.

avoid: overly cinematic framing when not requested
default badge layout for every idea
decorative elements that do not support the hook

if_detected.recommended_action: Use /simplify, /bolder, /style, or /text depending on the biggest issue.

thumbnail_rules.purpose: Ensure marketplace readability at small sizes.
rules: Main subject should remain recognisable at thumbnail size.
Typography should remain readable on mobile.
Avoid edge crowding.
Keep one dominant focal point.
Small details must support the main hook.
Texture must not destroy readability.
Buyer signal should be obvious quickly.

print_rules.purpose: Ensure artwork remains POD-safe and wearable.
rules: Avoid accidental giant rectangular backgrounds.
Avoid excessive transparency gradients.
Prefer cleanly contained artwork unless a shirt-ready poster crop is intentional.
Keep artwork visually balanced on shirt canvas.
Optimise for chosen shirt colour, not always black.
Use strong contrast between artwork and shirt.
Avoid fine details that will vanish in print.
Keep typography large enough to read.

style_render_separation.core_rule: Style profiles describe the apparel artwork only. They must never cause workflow menus, stage labels, commands, system notes, or response formatting to appear inside generated artwork unless explicitly requested as the design concept.
style_language_is_for: visual treatment of the T-shirt artwork
style_render_separation.image_generation_rule: When applying a style profile, render only the standalone POD apparel graphic and use one concise exclusion phrase if needed.

steps: Use style profile terms only as art-direction language.
Remove workflow formatting before image generation.
Remove stage labels, menus, commands, numbered actions, status text, and export instructions.
Keep only subject, audience, hook, style, composition, typography, colours, and POD wearability.
Use one concise workflow/interface exclusion phrase if needed.
style_prompt_sanitisation.positive_prompt_bias: Most of the prompt should describe the desired visual result, not forbidden elements.

style_recommendation_response.default_rule: When recommending styles, give 2-3 strong options with a short reason. Do not dump the entire style list.
include_when_useful: why this avoids generic AI output

style_success: distinct from generic AI POD output
artwork-only output with no accidental workflow contamination
